Soft dawn paints the pond  
One ripple disturbs the sky  
Silence blooms in light

Soft rain taps the leaves  
Morning light spills on still stone  
Day wakes without haste

Soft rain on pine leaves  
Morning light threads through the mist  
Silence learns to sing

Morning leans on the window glass,  
soft as breath and pale as rain.  
The world, half-dreaming, slowly wakes  
and gathers up its name again.

A sparrow stitches through the light,  
the kettle hums a silver tune,  
and somewhere in the quiet sky  
day opens like a paper moon.

Morning folds gold into the leaves,  
A quiet hymn the sidewalk keeps.  
The wind forgets, then softly grieves,  
And dusk arrives in violet sweeps.  

We pass like sparks through hours that roam,  
Brief as rain on window glass—  
Yet in one smile, one word, one home,  
A whole small universe can pass.

Morning pours gold on the waking street,  
Windows bloom softly, warm with light.  
A sparrow stitches the hush with song,  
And shadows fold up into flight.
Life is a library where every choice writes a new shelf into existence.
A metaphor is a shortcut for meaning: a bridge made of lightning.
The moon is a quiet librarian, shelving silver stories across the night sky.
